title: Is anyone on pump fun or bonk hitting quick scalps?
author: puttingupnumbers
selftext: Looking for advice on trading pump fun scalps

comments.author: sagesofthe4th_world
comments.body:
I do not. I mean it’s possible. Some traders do ok like this.

But I dont touch anything under 24 hours old or under 100K MC. Pretty decent PnL with this strategy. 

I typically hold from 1-2 days up to 2-3 weeks.

replies.author: puttingupnumbers
replies.body: youre on raydium then i assume? are you using like veloicty, volume, number of holders are volume?

replies.author: sagesofthe4th_world
replies.body: Exactly. Except I use the Jupiter API. I focus on trending tokens. I like new pairs just not that new.

replies.author: puttingupnumbers
replies.body: thats actually super interesting instead of pump portal. are you having good luck with it? can i pick your brain? I dont expect your exact edge and i can always share data and my findings. wont be offended if the answer is no. thanks man!

replies.author: sagesofthe4th_world
replies.body:
Yeah wouldn't call it luck only because that's how I used to play this game lol. Now just lots of back testing and data driven decisions.

For example new pairs under 24 hours old are always a huge rug risk. Regardless of their numbers/metrics. That eliminated a lot of rugs for me when trading.

The other big lesson was diversification.

Pumping memes will always be some kind of mysterious. You have to play the numbers a little. Some plays have great looking setups but never go anywhere, while others are a complete surprise i.e. some big influencer tweeted about it an hour ago and revived it.

Sure please DM any questions. I can share some of it.

Jupiter has the best data on Solana memes from what I've seen. I use their API bc I'm developing trade bot scripts but you could still do this old school just by look at their website's trending pairs.
